reasoning: In Q4 2024, before the Ukraine transit ended, the EU imported roughly 13.3 bcm of Russian gas (19% of 70 bcm total). In January 2025, the pipeline transit through Ukraine ended, which removed approximately 14-15 bcm/year (or ~3.5 bcm/quarter) of supply capacity. The remaining supply channels for Russian gas to the EU are primarily the TurkStream pipeline (max capacity ~15.75 bcm/year or ~4 bcm/quarter) and LNG shipments (historically ~18-20 bcm/year or ~5 bcm/quarter). Combined, this physical capacity maxes out around 9-10 bcm per quarter. To hit the 15 bcm target in any single quarter in 2025, Ukraine pipeline transit would need to be unexpectedly restarted and run at full capacity, alongside maximum volumes from TurkStream and LNG. The crowd forecast is 1%, reflecting these physical and political realities. Following the system constraints (0.05 to 0.95), I predict 5% (0.05).
